stemHtml: In which conditions would you most likely encounter clear icing, and how would it normally appear?

options.a: Cumuliform clouds, large water droplets, temperatures between 0°C and -15°C. Appears smooth and tends to spread back over an aircraft wing.
options.b: Cumuliform clouds, small water droplets, temperatures between -20°C and 25°C. Appears transparent and tends to take the shape of the surface on which it freezes.
options.c: Stratiform clouds, large water droplets, temperatures well below freezing. Appears opaque and builds forward from leading surface into a sharp edge.
options.d: Stratiform clouds, small water droplets, temperatures between -10°C and -20°C. Appears granular and tends to accumulate forward into the air stream.
